content:
Phase 3.3 – Connect Expo app image deletion to Cloudflare Worker

Current status:
- The Cloudflare Worker `heavyar-api` is already deployed and working
- Worker URL:
  https://heavyar-api.heavyar-official.workers.dev
- Verified endpoints:
  - GET /health
  - POST /cloudinary/delete
- Cloudinary upload from the Expo app already works correctly using the existing upload preset flow
- The app already stores Cloudinary image objects with:
  - url
  - publicId
- The current app baseline is working and must not be broken

Important constraints:
- Incremental changes only
- Do NOT refactor the app
- Do NOT change Firebase Auth, Firestore, roles, or equipment creation flow
- Do NOT change the existing Cloudinary upload flow
- Do NOT move any secrets into the app
- Do NOT add CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET anywhere in frontend code
- Keep the current architecture stable
- Do not perform unrelated cleanup

Your task:
Integrate image deletion in the Expo / React Native app so that Cloudinary assets are deleted through the Cloudflare Worker whenever appropriate.

Implementation requirements:

1. Add a minimal service/helper in the Expo app to call:
   POST https://heavyar-api.heavyar-official.workers.dev/cloudinary/delete

2. Request body must be:
   {
     "publicId": "<cloudinary public id>"
   }

3. Use the safest existing config/service location for the Worker base URL

4. Update equipment deletion flow so that:
   - when an equipment item is deleted,
   - the app extracts all Cloudinary image publicIds from the equipment document,
   - calls the Worker deletion endpoint for each valid publicId,
   - then deletes the Firestore document
   - if Cloudinary deletion partially fails, handle it safely and do not crash the UI

5. Update equipment image replacement/update flow so that:
   - if existing images are removed/replaced during update,
   - only the removed Cloudinary images are deleted via the Worker
   - retained images must not be touched

6. Preserve backward compatibility:
   - the app supports both legacy string image entries and object-based entries
   - only attempt Cloudinary deletion for image objects that contain a valid publicId

7. Add robust error handling:
   - no UI crash if Worker call fails
   - avoid blocking the whole app unnecessarily
   - keep the Firestore flow stable

8. Keep changes minimal and localized
   - no refactor
   - no styling changes unless absolutely required for this flow

Suggested integration points based on current project structure:
- expo/services/cloudinaryService.ts
- expo/services/firestoreService.ts
- any screen/form that triggers equipment update or deletion, only if necessary

After finishing, return:
1. Exact files changed
2. Short explanation of what changed
3. Whether real Cloudinary deletion through the Worker is now fully supported
4. How legacy image strings are handled
5. Any remaining limitation, if any
